title: Bumble Q1 2023 Earnings Call Summary
text: Bumble delivered strong Q1 2023 performance with total revenue of $243 million, growing 16% year-over-year and reaching the high end of guidance. The company achieved adjusted EBITDA of $59 million, representing a 24% margin that exceeded expectations. Total paying users increased 15% to 3.5 million. Average revenue per paying user (ARPPU) grew to $22.83. Bumble app specifically added 98,000 sequential net paying users. CEO Whitney Wolfe Herd noted that revenue growth was fueled by both payers and ARPPU, with Bumble app revenue reaching $194 million, up 26% year-over-year. The app maintained strong market positions globally, ranking as the most downloaded dating app in the U.K. and Canada during Q1. The company continued gaining market share across Western Europe, Latin America, and India despite elevated competitive marketing spend. Key initiatives include full rollout of Compliments feature, with nearly 15% of monthly active users adopting it. Best Bees AI-powered curation offering expanding across Western European markets. Testing of new Gen Z-focused subscription tier priced below Bumble Boost. For Q2 2023, management expects total revenue between $254-258 million (17% growth at midpoint) and Bumble app revenue of $205-208 million (23% growth). Sequential net adds of 120,000-130,000 for Bumble app expected. Adjusted EBITDA of $62-64 million with 25% margin.
